comment: Thunderhead rules metadata (plan 5e: the SVG carries shape, this file carries scoring/mode rules). Consumed by src/game/Scoring.ts and src/game/thunderhead.ts. Design truth: design/tables/thunderhead/BRIEF.md. GREY-BOX PROTOTYPE values - tuned for the stagger feel gate, not final balance.

gale.comment: G-A-L-E completions step the bonus multiplier (persistent letters, lane change) and add charge. Bonus units are BAROGRAPH INCHES.

charge.comment: Vane spins + G-A-L-E completions build charge; at cellCharge a CHARGE CELL lights (alternating). A lit cell grabs a passing ground ball, holds a beat and FLINGS it - toward the lit STORM ROUTE shot when the route is running, outward otherwise.

route.comment: STORM ROUTE: a cell fling opens a lit-shot chain (spine -> circuit -> nacelle) on routeS per step; completing the chain banks a STRIKE.

ballast.comment: Dropping all three BALLAST targets sheds ballast: the ship climbs - ballastS of x scoreFactor with both outlane saves lit.

spine.comment: SPINE RUN: left-flipper climb up the envelope flank, airborne release over the cells. Pays from the leave-at-the-top event; boarded-at-the-mouth guarded.

nacelle.comment: Instrument scoop ladder (wrapping); the top rung lights LIGHTNING WATCH.

lightning.comment: LIGHTNING WATCH (DMD video mode): nacelle ladder top lights it; the next nacelle capture holds the ball while strikes charge in a quadrant - call it with the matching flipper (left = port, right = starboard) before it lands. Timer-driven (sim-safe).

squall.comment: SQUALL: strikesForSquall banked STRIKES start a timed 3-ball; both cells run continuously (the storm aboard), cell-fling jackpots, double at the spine.

eye.comment: THE EYE (wizard): a squall survived + two storm routes + galeMax multiplier light the nacelle; 30 s of dead calm - everything lit, x scoreFactor, cells hold-and-place (the storm helping).

skill.comment: STORM GLASS (differentiation-pass pattern): a soft plunge peaking in the sensor-skill-stormglass band (fires at <= maxSpeed m/s) pays points and spots one G-A-L-E lane. Once per ball.
